what_it_does: This file is a noticeboard request item that records a follow-up task to promote attachment_type from a standalone runtime registry into a first-class _CORPUS_REGISTRIES kind alongside item_type and source_type.
observations: Follow-up to item-e523b30d after channel-attachment was built at commit 385a71b
runtime/attachment_types.AttachmentTypeRegistry already works standalone and validates attachment_type fail-loud
The request asks to wire it into suite.py _CORPUS_REGISTRIES, add create_* authoring, expose it in cognition_info, and update AGENTS.md
Explicitly deferred out of the fork's lane to avoid concurrent suite.py edits / committer-collision
No data migration is needed because the registry already functions
standouts: Deferred to the lead's single 'first-classness' pass to avoid committer collision
The registry is already operational; the work is about authorability and visibility
Scope is tightly bounded: one registry row, create_* path, cognition_info, AGENTS.md
conventions: Uses noticeboard YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, state, title, author_session, links, and history
References related items with board:// and session:// URI links
Request type with state 'open' and a single history transition from null to open
concerns: Deferral implies coordination dependency that is not tracked by an explicit blocked_by link
No acceptance criteria or validation steps are listed
notes: A deferred follow-up request to make attachment_type a first-class corpus registry kind, scoped to avoid suite.py merge conflicts.
questions: What is the exact row shape expected in _CORPUS_REGISTRIES for attachment_type?
What does the create_* authoring path concretely require in suite.py?
What specific AGENTS.md section or heading is the 'drift-home'?
What validation schema does AttachmentTypeRegistry enforce?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches the file: it is a request to promote attachment_type to a first-class registry kind, and the content describes exactly that scope.
intention_signals: It is trying to make attachment types as creatable, discoverable, and integrated as item_type and source_type within the corpus registry system.
novelty: The explicit deferral to a lead-owned 'first-classness' pass because of committer-collision risk, rather than completing it immediately on the fork.
contribution: Captures the intent, scope, and rationale for integrating attachment_type into the corpus registry suite, including the deliberate sequencing decision and the no-migration boundary.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard request item proposing to promote the runtime AttachmentTypeRegistry to a first-class _CORPUS_REGISTRIES kind with authoring and cognition_info visibility.
intention_for_embedding: To schedule and scope the registry integration work so attachment_type becomes authorable and visible alongside item_type and source_type without migration risk.
